characters.name: Abdul
characters.description: AbdulCharacter informationOther namesAbdul the Brass and Food MerchantOccupationMerchant (Sells cheese, cookware, drinks, falafels, lettuce, poppy-seed pita covered in Special Sauce and snacks)HomeAgrabahSourceAbdulwas a brass and cookware merchant that lived inAgrabah. He seems somewhat welcoming towards guests and will try to flatter them, however he is one to hold a grudge as according toAladdin, Abdul still hates his guts for stealing from him in the past despite Aladdin having turned over a new leaf. AladdinandAbuused to steal things from him, especially Abu, which he never forgave them for.

characters.name: Abis Mal
characters.description: Abis MalCharacter informationOther namesEl Fatal (German Dub)[1]The Fat One (byDouani) (The Flawed Couple)OccupationThiefGang LeaderAlignmentBadAffiliationsAbis Mal's ThugsThieves GuildRelativesAbnor Mal(ancestor)Unnamed mother (Mentioned inSmolder and WiserandThe Flawed Couple)EnemiesAladdin,Jasmine,Genie,Abu,Magic Carpet,Iago,Razoul,Mother GriffinLikesRiches, the Sultan's "big hat"DislikesLosing riches, Aladdin, humiliation, defeat, being called stupid, his plans being foiled or considered "ill-conceived", his henchmen failing or turning on himPowers and abilitiesSword proficiencyAccess to magic itemsParaphernaliaScimitarVarious mystical artifactsSource qwcvtnyyuklo Abis Malis the secondary antagonist of Disney’s Aladdin franchise, serving as the secondary antagonist of the direct-to-video filmThe Return of Jafarand one of the main antagonists ofAladdintelevision series.

characters.name: Abis Mal's Thugs
characters.description: Abis Mal's ThugsBackground informationVoiceJeff BennettFrank WelkerJim CummingsRob PaulsenDan CastellanetaCharacter informationEnemiesAladdinIagoJasmineAbuGenieThe SultanRazoulRoyal GuardsMagic CarpetParaphernaliaSwordsDaggersClubsSourceAbis Mal's Thugsare minor villains/antagonists in the animated sequel,The Return of Jafar, andAladdin (TV series). The bandits are first shown during the opening of the movie returning from a successful raid onAgrabah, with apparently little to no help fromAbis Malhimself. Nonetheless, Mal claims the majority of the loot as a combined "beloved leader bonus" whilst leaving the rest of his troop with a small bag of coins as their share. Their anger at their stingy boss is cut short by the shocking sight of the chest of some of their loot seemingly moving on its own. One thief lifts up the chest crossing his path to reveal it being Abu who was carrying it.Abis Malgrabs Abu as he tries to run away, only for Aladdin come from nowhere to the monkey's defense, jumping on four of the thugs heads in the process. Mal orders his men to attack him and they do so in the form of a brief brawl cloud, which reveals them pummeling one of their own in the place of Aladdin once subsiding. The crooks chase Aladdin and Abu as they steal back the treasure they took, but are consistently outwitted along the way. The next day, three of the henchmen also appeared with Abis Mal at the marketplace where they attempted to exact revenge on Aladdin (even reminding their leader of who he was in minor annoyance when he failed to remember who he was), although they were ultimately forced to flee after Iago not only sent them flying into a cart of eggs, but the guards also recognized them, or rather, their leader for his prior thefts. They eventually tried to kill him at the well both for the failure and for his stinginess, although his unknowingly summoning Jafar from his lamp forced them to flee. Despite apparently betraying him before, the thieves that followAbis Malappear as more obedient and somewhat more cowardly henchmen, as he would kill or punish them severely if they failed him, though at least two episodes show that they are still prone to turning against him depending on the circumstance. Despite appearing somewhat frequently however, Mal's henchmen are also shown to have limited involvement in his plans on the show, indicating that they aren't fully at his disposal, as they only aid him in four of the 13 episodes that he is the antagonist of, and are more often shown without his presence or conjunction, typically at the Skull and Dagger, the cafe headquarters of Agrabah's criminals and other rogues. In fact, it's implied that their allegiance isn't limited to each other or Abis Mal himself, asone episodehasone such henchmanpaired up withDouani, a thief with no personal affiliation with Mal.

characters.name: Abnor Mal
characters.description:
Abnor MalBackground informationVoiceMark L. TaylorCharacter informationOccupationGoat herder, Tentmaker, Nomad, and Abis Mal's ancestorAlignmentBadRelativesAbis Mal's mother (descendant-in-law) (Mentioned inSmolder and WiserandThe Flawed Couple)Abis Mal(descendant)EnemiesSheik Hamed,Jasmine,Aladdin,Genie,Carpet,Abu,IagoLikesBeing an elephant driver, big hats, tents, riches, getting what he wants, and winningDislikesNo tents, not getting what he wants, losing, and people standing in his waySource Abnor resembles Abis, except he doesn't have a goatee. He is also 
dressed like Abis Mal: the turban and the arabian outfit, but their in 
different colors. Initially, he seems nice but bossy and fearful of losing his position as tent maker. He does show slight arrogance and capabilities of manipulation, making sure that Hamed wouldn't listen to Aladdin and laughing at Aladdin's suggestion Agrabah could be built. While harmless, he showed a darker side to his personality as he began to like Abis Mal's idea of overthrowing Hamed, becoming a thief, dictator, tyrant, and traitor.

characters.name: Abu
characters.description: AbuBackground informationFeature filmsAladdin (film)The Return of JafarAladdin and the King of ThievesMore than a Peacock PrincessAladdin (2019 film)Television programsAladdinVideo gamesAladdin (video game)Nasira's RevengeDisney's Math Quest With AladdinDisney's Reading Quest with AladdinVoiceFrank WelkerCharacter informationOther namesChief (by Genie)Chihuahua (by Iago)[1]Esteemed Effendi (by Aladdin)Friend (by Aladdin inJasmine: The Missing Coin)Hairy little thief (by Aladdin)Monkey (by Iago)Monkey Boy (by Genie)MonkeytownMy friend (by Jasmine inThe Jewel Orchard)One moldy monkey (by Iago)Pal (by Aladdin inThe Pharaoh's CurseandMoving Daze)Shaggy (by Genie)Sultan(by Aladdin, Farouk and Jasmine)The Chimp (by Genie)You little rascal (by Aladdin inThe Jewel Orchard)OccupationThief (formerly)AlignmentGoodEnemiesJafarIago(formerly)Razoul(formerly)Abis MalHaroud Hazi BinMirageMozenrathMechaniclesAyam AghoulSa'LukLikesFruits, vegetables, foods, shiny objects, money, tricking the guards, Aladdin's friendshipDislikesDanger, fireworks, flying, Aladdin scolding him, poverty, the royal guards, Jasmine (formerly)Powers and abilitiesNatural acrobaticsExpertise in thieverySourceAbuisAladdin's pet monkey and friend, alongsideGenieandCarpet. He is a small and slender copper tufted/vervet monkey with a red vest, red above with a purple bottom fez. He is scheming and sneaky and hates danger. However, he is also very loyal to Aladdin and is willing to risk his life to save him and his friends. He tends to be Iago's partner in crime, but is less deceitful and more honest and trustworthy than Iago is. Like Iago, Abu can get frustrated rather quickly and hates to be made a fool of, especially by someone as close as Aladdin. And he is also known as astute, big-hearted, unselfish, thoughtful, helpful, efficient, mischievous, optimistic, nice, kind-hearted, energetic, feisty, adventurous, benevolent, unswerving, tidy, humble, empathetic, mild-mannered, open-minded, naive, keen, emotional and sensitive.

characters.name: Abuddin
characters.description: AbuddinBackground informationFeature filmsAladdin and the King of ThievesCharacter informationAffiliationsForty ThievesSourceAbuddinis one of the minor characters inAladdin and the King of Thieves. He is one of the animals who was making his way towardAgrabah. Abuddin and the other camels come up the hill behindCassim.Cassimand the other camels make their way towardAgrabah. He is later seen somewhere in the courtyard of Agrabah, while the two of theForty Thievesin his baskets pop out and burst into their song,There's a Party Here in Agrabah. Abuddin is later seen walking with the other camels to the stables of Agrabah. WhenCassimknocks on one of the baskets, theForty Thievesemerge from the baskets. He is not seen again for the rest of the film. Abuddin is a large tan camel with gray eyes, a white harness around his neck and his snout, a violet harness around his body, and carries light gray baskets on his body.

characters.name: Ajed Al-Gebraic
characters.description: Ajed Al-GebraicBackground informationVoiceJonathan HarrisCharacter informationOther namesAl-GebraicGebOccupationGenie's old masterAlignmentBad, later GoodHomeA little cottage on a cliff, somewhere in the high mountainsLikesGetting what he wantsDislikesNot getting what he wants, not being eternally youthful, and having made a mistake with the eternal dealPowers and abilitiesImmortalitySource Behind his gentle senile old man facade lies a greedy and selfish man who sees Genie as a servant, not a friend. When Genie is being hunted by the Muktar, Aladdin assumes it to be one of his old masters. Genie remarks all his masters are dead but then remembers that he was traded by Ajed for immortality. They arrive at his little cottage on a cliff, high in the mountains. When Aladdin knocks on the door, he answers and is surprised to see Genie. At first, Genie is angry at Ajed for trading him to a sorcerer in return for eternal life but Ajed says he should've asked for eternal youth as well and invites everyone in. He quickly wins Genie's trust but Aladdin begins to suspect something is off about him as he tries to prevent them from leaving, only to learn Ajed was stalling for time until the Muktar came to capture Genie and make him his personal servant. However, after seeing Aladdin sacrifice himself for Genie, he becomes disgusted by the act and says he will wait until Aladdin dies before trying to capture Genie again, not knowing the Muktar will be retired by then.

characters.description: AkbarBackground informationVoiceJim CummingsCharacter informationOccupationThief (formerly)Do gooder/Donater (currently)AlignmentBad, later GoodHomeAgrabahSourceAkbaris a former thief turned missionary of good who lives inAgrabah. Do gooder/Donater (currently) He is a large thief with a strange fez and wears grey clothing with a brown vest.

characters.name: Akim
characters.description: AkimCharacter informationHomeGanastanSourceAkimis the ambassador ofGanastan. Akim had been picked by the ruler of Ganastan to fortify the alliance betweenAgrabahand Ganastan, and to ensure everything went well,Aladdinpersonally escorted Akim to Agrabah to ensure he would have a pleasant time. Upon arriving, he was informed thatThe Sultanhad managed to defeat the dreadedDominus Tuskwho had laid siege to Ganastan once before. Akim was impressed and marveled at the Sultan's feat, however he was unaware that the Sultan was under the control of the evil spirit ofKileemwho was not amused by Akim's words and instead threatened him and Ganastan with war for his "insolence", nearly breaking his hand in the process. Akim is not seen again after that, and because of this, it is unknown if Akim and Ganastan severed ties with Agrabah.

characters.name: Al Muddy
characters.description: Al MuddyBackground informationVoiceFrank WelkerCharacter informationOccupationServants of the Al Muddy SultanAffiliationsAl Muddy Sultan(ruler)HomeDeep beneath the desertSubterranean World of the Al MuddyPowers and abilitiesMalleable mud-like bodiesSourceTheAl Muddyare a race of subterranean carnivorous cannibalistic earthelementalsruled by the evil and giganticAl Muddy Sultan. They seem unintelligent and only interested in their next meal but are also fierce warriors and can be surprisingly quick with trying to capture their enemies. The Al Muddy are vaguely humanoid entities made entirely of mud. Because of their slimy composition, the Al Muddy can potentially take any form they want, such as amorphous blobs or evenCentaurs, but they mainly prefer their humanoid forms. Their faces are relatively humanoid as well but they have smaller skulls than humans along with dark red eyes, bulbous noses and large mouths. All Al Muddy with the exception of their giant leader look exactly the same and have an overall masculine appearance, with no females shown to exist, possibly due to that Al Muddy can reproduce via subdividing. Because of their primitive nature, the Al Muddy do not have a proper society or culture. Only lounging about in mud or day and thinking up new ways to get their next meal. The only sign of culture in their entire realm is the palace of their Sultan, whose intelligence has allowed him to learn and adopt several human habits and even form complex architecture for himself. Because of this, he comes off as more cultured than his subjects with a mentality focused on luxury and fine dining, coming off as somewhat of a gourmet with an appreciation for high quality food, although whether all of these traits are shared by the rest of the Al Muddy race as they grow bigger and more intelligent or if its exclusive to their sultan is unknown.

characters.name: Al Muddy Sultan
characters.description: Al Muddy SultanBackground informationVoiceJim Cummings,Frank WelkerCharacter informationOther namesDer Schlamanensultan (The Slammer Sultan) (German Dub)[1]Sludgy (by Aladdin)Wolfgang Muck (by Iago)OccupationSultan of the Al MuddyRuler of theAl MuddyraceAlignmentBadAffiliationsAl Muddy(subjects)HomeDeep beneath the desert (formerly)Al Muddy PalaceLikesCooking his victimsDislikesIntense heat of the desert's sunPowers and abilitiesSuperhuman strength and durabilityMalleabilityLeadership intuitionSourceTheAl Muddy Sultanis an evil giant mud entity and the ruler of theAl Muddyrace. He resembled the standard Al Muddy but what made him stand out from others of his kind was his titanic size, easily towering over humans and camels which looked like insects compared to him. Other than this, he was also the only Al Muddy with pupils and the largest head, a physical display of his higher intelligence and sapience. Aside from his size and might, the Sultan also stood out from his people for his great cunning and sophisticated intelligence. He was also capable of human speech and a fondness for cooking unlike his more feral followers.

characters.name: Al-Hakeem
characters.description: Al-HakeemBackground informationFeature filmsAladdinTelevision programsAladdinVideo gamesNasira's RevengeVoiceFrank WelkerCharacter informationOther namesHakeemFire-BreatherFire-EaterOccupationFakirAffiliationsAgrabah MarketplaceHomeAgrabahSourceAl-Hakeemor simply"The Fire Breather"is a fakir who does street performances in the streets ofAgrabah. He is a tall and lanky individual with pale skin, balding hair, a hooked nose and a skinny frame who only wears a simple white turban and shorts. His most notable trait though are his peculiar pointed ears. In the animated series, his turban's color was changed to red.

characters.name: Ali Baba
characters.description: Ali BabaBackground informationFeature filmsAladdinSource Ali Babawas a heroic individual from the past who fought against a previous incarnation of theForty Thieves. Little is known about Ali Baba except that he fought and presumably defeated a past incarnation of theForty Thievesand his adventure was later magically preserved in story by the legendary queenScheherazade. It was either before or around this time that a ship that contained the legendaryHand of Midassank in the waterways of the hideout of the Forty Thieves and a mural explaining the secrets of the Hand were carved within the deepest chambers of the hideout by an unknown individual or group, although whether Ali Baba had anything to do with this remains unknown.

Alicia is a young tall Arabian girl with short black hair and warm brown eyes.

characters.name: Amail
characters.description: AmailBackground informationFeature filmsAladdinThe Return of JafarVoiceCorey BurtonCharacter informationOther namesNecklace MerchantOccupationMerchantAffiliationsAgrabah MarketplaceHomeAgrabahSourceAmailis a jewelry merchant inAgrabah. Amail is a smooth-talker who flatters his customers in order to entice them into buying his wares. He also enjoys street performers, findingAzizto be quite entertaining, not because of his "talent" but rather because the hilarity of watching him fumble. Amail making a sales pitch to Jasmine.

characters.name: Amail

characters.name: Amal
characters.description: AmalBackground informationVoiceJim CummingsCharacter informationOccupationEl Khatib (formerly)Traveler and Do GooderAlignmentBad, later GoodHomeAgrabahMorbia(Formerly)Shadow Realm  (Formerly)EnemiesMirage,El KhatibAladdin, (formerly)Abu, (formerly)Iago, (formerly)Genie, (formerly)Magic Carpet(formerly)LikesHis friend Aladdin (currently)DislikesAladdin (formerly) and being a Shadow-WalkerPowers and abilitiesPhenomenal strength, teleporting through shadows, walk on walls, and deflect magicSourceAmalwas a native ofAgrabahandAladdin's best friend, but vanished when he and Aladdin were children. As a boy he wore grey clothing consisting of a small grey fez and tattered grey shirt with shorts. He had shaggy hair like Aladdin and was scrawnier and had darker skin than Aladdin. As a Shadow Walker, Amal appears to be large with mottle-green fur, cat-like eyes and long ears. He also has claws and sharp, canine fangs.

characters.name: Amin Damoola
characters.description: Amin Damoolais a clumsy thief and a recurring comedic villain fromAladdinTV series. Amin Damoola is a klutzy thief, who appears in four episodes. Amin always finds a way to cause trouble forAladdin, usually ending up in trouble himself. Some of these ways are stealing a magical glove, creating a large green dragon, and "acquiring" a number of magical tools fromMozenrath. In his next appearance, he was working for Mozenrath and failed him; Mozenrath had given Amin a choice: succeed in stealing the Sultan and become the greatest thief of Agrabah or face Mozenrath's anger and regret the day he was born. Mozenrath did not take that too well, and it is likely that Amin was arrested by Aladdin and his friends. In his last appearance, he was seen trying to escape Aladdin and the Royal guards after having stolen a diamond until he was defeated by Genie and taken away by the guards to the dungeon. Amin appeared in four episodes:

characters.name: Amin's Dragon
characters.description: Amin's DragonCharacter informationEnemiesSquirt, (formerly)Iago, (formerly) andAmin DamoolaSourceTheDragonis a magical creature that appears in one the episode "Scare Necessities" inAladdin (TV series). It was conjured up bySquirtwhen he was scared and served asAmin'sbeast. He scared all the thieves out of the den andIago, andAbuwent inside. Iago cornered Amin and tried to save Squirt, but the Dragon appeared behind him.

characters.name: Amuk Moonrah
characters.description: Amuk MoonrahBackground informationVoiceTim CurryCharacter informationOccupationFormer Ancient Ruler of AgrabahPowerful Ancient BeingFire DemonAlignmentBadHomeThe mountains (prison)EnemiesFasir,Iago,Aladdin,Genie,Jasmine,Abu,CarpetLikesDarkness, fire, fireworks, tributes, sacrifices, and the suffering of othersDislikesThe sun, sunlight, and the amulet that can destroy himPowers and abilitiesAble to turn into smoke and fire, create fireballs, immense strength, and drawing power from fireSourceAmuk Moonrahwas an ancient and powerful demon of pure evil who once ruled over Agrabah thousands of years ago. He is a large 20 foot tall demon lion with red fur yellow eyes grey wings of a eagle instead of bat-like wings, and has hind legs and tail. He wears a purple loincloth. He is a cruel leader who is unreasonable and enjoys causing others misery. He is selfish and demands sacrifices simply for the joy of taking things from people, and is sadistic as he took a boy's teddy bear just to make him cry and chained up the princess.

characters.name: Aneesa
characters.description: AneesaBackground informationVoiceFlo Di RieCharacter informationOccupationLady in WaitingAlignmentGoodHomeAgrabahThe Sultan's PalaceLikesHelping JasmineSourceAneesais Princess Jasmine's Lady-in-Waiting who appears in the direct-to-video filmDisney Princess Enchanted Tales: Follow Your Dreams. She is a midly obese woman most likely in her middle age and wears a head scarf and brown dress with brown sash. She is a kind motherly figure for Jasmine. She is very light hearted and sweet but can also be clever and wise.

characters.name: Anis
characters.description: AnisBackground informationFeature filmsAladdin and the King of ThievesCharacter informationAffiliationsForty ThievesSourceAnisis one of the minor characters inAladdin and the King of Thieves. He is also one of the animals who was making his way toward Agrabah. Anis and the other camels come up the hill behindCassim.Cassimand the other camels make their way towardAgrabah. He is later seen somewhere in the courtyard of Agrabah, while the two of theForty Thievesin his baskets pop out and burst into their song,There's a Party Here in Agrabah. Anis is later seen walking with the other camels to the stables of Agrabah. WhenCassimknocks on one of the baskets, theForty Thievesemerge from the baskets. He is not seen again for the rest of the film. Anis is a large copper camel with gray eyes, a blue harness around his neck, a dark brown harness around his snout and his body, and carries golden tan baskets on his body.

characters.name: Anubis
characters.description:
AnubisBackground informationVideo gamesNasira's RevengeVoiceFrank WelkerCharacter informationOccupationJackal, Egyptian God, DogAlignmentBadHomeEgyptEnemiesAladdinJasmineIagoAbuGenieMagic CarpetThe SultanDislikesResurrections, favoritismPowers and abilitiesDivine powers over life and deathSourceAnubisis a giant statue Egyptian jackal-headed god of the Underworld at The Great Pyramid, the biggest of any other pyramids, a minor villain as well as a boss figure character. He first appears in Disney's AladdinNasira's Revenge: The Video Game. whose sorcery is the source of the restorative magic of fourGolden Serpent Idols, the very 
artifacts sought after byNasirain her plan of resurrect her twin brotherJafarfrom the dead. He guards inside The Great Pyramid where Princess Jasmine,The Sultan's daughter is being held.AladdinentersThe Great Pyramidwhere he finds PrincessJasminetrapped inside. Anubis comes to life and fights him,Aladdinbeats him and Anubis lifts away. Anubis was a dark blue jackal head god statue of The Underworld with his staff and white short. Category;Adults
